# A0/A1 J11-J22 Simultaneous Correction Quality

## J1 Means

| variant | dx err after | theta err after | |V| err after | P residual after | Q residual after | correction/full | correction wall ms |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
|A0|0.0663271895163|0.0591446000177|0.74906793629|0.333867197433|0.123663827297|0.949430961434|0.73621266|
|A1|0.0499948252375|0.0461010703415|0.109718305196|0.199305751889|0.0320594082521|1.28866259903|1.17739724|

## Stage 1 Judgment

- `A0` solves J11 and J22 from the same residual, then applies both corrections simultaneously.
- `A1` reuses the same J11/J22 factors and solves one extra cross-residual round.
- `A1` improves dx error over `A0` on `6/10` rows, theta error on `6/10` rows, and |V| error on `7/10` rows.
- Cost gate: J1 mean correction/full ratios are listed above. Near-1 means the correction is close to full cuDSS factor+solve cost before adding BiCGSTAB(2).
- Hybrid Stage 2 may proceed for the cheaper variant if it improves dx quality without reintroducing large P/Q residuals.
